correction.rationale: Use positive points centered within each visible toe and along the distal toe region, with negative points in the proximal dorsal forefoot/metatarsal area. SAM refinement is safer than a coarse polygon because the five toe separations and the MTP boundary are curved and partially subtle.

judgments.evidence: The target side is correct per semantic evidence, and all five visible toes are included. The localized defect is the proximal boundary: it crosses into the forefoot instead of following the visible foot-MTP line.

observations.contour: The cyan contour follows the outer dorsal and plantar silhouette reasonably well, but the proximal contour does not follow the anatomical MTP split and is overly proximal.
observations.full_context: The semantic pose-chain identifies this visible foot as the character's right foot; the target is the forward foot with toes extending toward the viewer-right.
observations.mask: The mask is a single connected component covering all visible toes, but its nearly vertical proximal boundary is too far back and includes a substantial portion of the forefoot/metatarsal region rather than starting at the foot-MTP line.
observations.neighbor_overlap: The mask directly abuts and partially includes the neighboring right foot-base/metatarsal region at the proximal boundary; no background or clothing contamination is apparent.
observations.overlay: The red overlay covers the toes completely but extends proximally over dorsal forefoot skin. Distal toe edges appear generally aligned with the visible silhouette.
observations.source_crop: The crop clearly shows five visible right toes, with an anatomical transition from the metatarsal/forefoot into the toes. The proximal forefoot is visually distinct from the distal toes.

correction.rationale: Mask boundary and coverage are anatomically correct for right_toes; no correction needed.

judgments.evidence: The mask correctly isolates the right toes from the foot base along the MTP boundary, with coverage matching visible toe pixels and no contamination from neighboring foot_base or background.

observations.contour: Contour tightly traces the toe cluster boundary including individual toe curvature at the tips.
observations.full_context: The cyan ROI box highlights the forward foot's toe region, consistent with the right foot per pose-chain evidence (nearest_semantic_chain=right).
observations.mask: Mask covers the toe cluster region only, triangular shape extending from mid-foot to toe tips, excluding the heel/ankle area.
observations.neighbor_overlap: Magenta overlap shows mask confined to toes with no bleed into foot_base or ankle region, small gap at top consistent with MTP line.
observations.overlay: Red overlay aligns well with the visible toes, following the MTP boundary line separating toes from foot base.
observations.source_crop: High-res crop shows a foot with toes extending to the right, ankle and midfoot visible on the left side.
